summary: All 10 mandatory TDD anchors from task 9 are present (plus one useful regression pin), fakes are hand-rolled per patterns.md, Dispatchers.setMain + StandardTestDispatcher is correctly used, and the PopBack event test handles subscription timing correctly with CoroutineStart.UNDISPATCHED. However, two major litmus-test weaknesses need fixing before merge: (1) renameChatWithBlankTitleResetsAutoTitle does not actually pin the AutoTitleGenerator path — any non-blank string with isManuallyTitled=false would pass, even a dumb constant or the pre-existing manual title; (2) date-grouping has only one sample per bucket so the 2..6 boundary and the 'local midnight, not 24h rolling' guarantee (explicitly called out in Decision 7 / buildSections comments) are not regression-pinned. Additional gaps: AC-U1's 60-character cap on manual titles is not exercised, and no empty-list case is pinned.

findings.issue: The assertion only checks `call.isManuallyTitled == false` and `call.title.isNotBlank()`. This passes against a broken implementation that ignores AutoTitleGenerator entirely — e.g. if the VM replaces the blank-branch with `updateChatTitle(chatId, chat.title ?: "x", isManuallyTitled = false)`, the test still passes because the seeded ChatEntity has `title = "Old manual"` which is non-blank. The test is supposed to pin 'blank input regenerates auto-title from the first user message', but currently does not verify that AutoTitleGenerator was actually consulted.
findings.recommendation: Assert on the exact auto-title produced by AutoTitleGenerator for the seeded inputs. For `firstUserText = "Привет, как дела?"` (17 chars, ≤ MAX_LEN=20, no whitespace-runs), AutoTitleGenerator.generateTitle returns "Привет, как дела?" unchanged. Replace the loose `assertTrue(call.title.isNotBlank())` with `assertEquals("Привет, как дела?", call.title)`. For defence-in-depth also add a second case where no user messages exist (remove the `messageDao.put(...)` seed) and assert the fallback `"Чат от DD.MM HH:mm"` prefix: `assertTrue(call.title.startsWith("Чат от "))`. Both branches of AutoTitleGenerator (first-user-text slice AND fallback) are then pinned.

findings.issue: Only one sample per section (0 / 1 / 3 / 10 days). The strict `2L..6L` boundary in `classifyDate` is not regression-pinned: changing it to `1L..6L` (yesterday would fall into THIS_WEEK), `2L..7L` (day-7 should be EARLIER but would slip into THIS_WEEK), or `3L..6L` (day-2 falls into EARLIER) would still pass the existing suite. Additionally, tests use LocalTime.NOON as the seed timestamp, so the 'boundaries are by local date, not 24h rolling' guarantee documented in Decision 7 (and in the VM KDoc) is never tested — a message from 23:59 yesterday should land in YESTERDAY at 00:01 today.
findings.recommendation:
Add boundary samples:
- `day=2` → THIS_WEEK (pins lower edge of the range)
- `day=6` → THIS_WEEK (pins upper edge)
- `day=7` → EARLIER (pins off-by-one on the upper edge)
And a local-midnight guarantee test that seeds a chat with `lastMessageAt = atStartOfDayEpoch(today.minusDays(1)) + TimeUnit.HOURS.toMillis(23) + TimeUnit.MINUTES.toMillis(59)` (i.e. 23:59 local yesterday) and fixes `clock = { today }`, then asserts the chat lands in YESTERDAY (not TODAY). A single multi-chat test is fine: seed {0,1,2,6,7,10} day offsets and assert one `assertEquals` per section listing the expected ids in order — catches every off-by-one in one test.

findings.issue: AC-U1 mandates a 60-character cap (`.take(MAX_MANUAL_TITLE_LEN)` in the VM), but no test exercises it. Removing `.take(60)` in the implementation would not fail any existing test.
findings.recommendation: Add `renameChatWithLongTitleTruncatesTo60Chars`: call `viewModel.renameChat(id, "a".repeat(100))`, assert `chatRepo.titleUpdates.single().title.length == 60` and `call.isManuallyTitled == true`. Also worth adding a boundary case at exactly 60 chars (not truncated, no trailing modification).

findings.issue: No test pins the empty-list contract (`if (chats.isEmpty()) return emptyList()` in buildSections). AC for drawer ('Empty state: Нет сохранённых чатов.') relies on `sections.isEmpty()` being observable from the VM layer. A stray change that returns four empty sections instead of an empty list would not be caught.
findings.recommendation: Add `buildsEmptySectionsWhenNoChats`: start with `chatRepo.emitChats(emptyList())` (default), call `subscribedViewModel()`, advanceUntilIdle(), assert `viewModel.drawerUiState.value.sections.isEmpty()` AND `viewModel.drawerUiState.value.isLoading == false`. Small but pins the UI-visible contract that DrawerContent switches on.

findings.issue: No test pins the `last_message_at DESC` order within a section (AC-P4: 'чаты отсортированы по last_message_at DESC'). `buildSections` performs the sort, but the existing per-section tests each seed a single chat so ordering cannot regress visibly.
findings.recommendation: Add `ordersChatsWithinSectionByLastMessageAtDesc`: seed three chats on `today` with different last_message_at values (e.g. today noon, today 10:00, today 14:00), assert TODAY section's `chats.map { it.id }` matches the expected DESC order. Catches an accidental `sortedBy` or removal of the sort.

findings.location: app/src/test/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/ui/drawer/DrawerViewModelTest.kt:250-257 (checkModelAvailableReturnsFalseWhenChatUnknown) and the two preceding checkModelAvailable tests
findings.issue: checkModelAvailable reads from `drawerUiState.value.sections` internally, so these tests depend on the `subscribedViewModel` helper launching a background collector (`backgroundScope.launch { viewModel.drawerUiState.collect {} }`) AND on `advanceUntilIdle()` having drained the initial combine emission. This couples the test to an implementation detail of `stateIn(WhileSubscribed)`. The helper is fine, but future refactors (e.g. changing `checkModelAvailable` to look up via ChatDao instead of uiState) would silently break these tests without any assertion change.
findings.recommendation: Option A (preferred): expose a dedicated lookup that doesn't go through uiState — have checkModelAvailable resolve the chat's modelId via `chatDao.getById(chatId)?.modelId` directly from its suspend form (or keep current impl but accept this coupling). Option B (minimum): add a KDoc comment on `subscribedViewModel()` stating the collector is required to populate uiState; and add an explicit pre-assertion `assertTrue(viewModel.drawerUiState.value.sections.isNotEmpty())` in the two SUCCEEDED / NOT_DOWNLOADED tests so the failure mode is diagnosable if the helper ever changes.

findings.issue: `checkModelAvailable` has a third branch: chat exists in uiState but `registry.models` has no matching entry (model removed from allowlist — explicitly described in the edge-cases section of task 9). It should return `false` (not NPE). No test covers it.
findings.recommendation: Add `checkModelAvailableReturnsFalseWhenModelNotInRegistry`: seed a chat with `modelId = "ghost"` and leave the registry empty (`registry.emit(emptyList())`). Assert `viewModel.checkModelAvailable(chatId) == false`. Pins the `entry?.downloadStatus?.status == SUCCEEDED` null-safety on the registry lookup, not just the chatId lookup.
